question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: roundDownToHour(long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coarse 1-hour granularity of roundDownToHour() to ensure that a throttleDefinitionFile update that increases TPS limits is not reflected in block processing for up to 3600 seconds, during which the old restrictive throttle definitions cause block times to exceed 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the lazy initialization of getSystemFiles() during a period when the database contains a corrupted throttleDefinitionFile, causing loadAll() to store a genesis-era fallback throttle file in the map, and since the map is never refreshed, all subsequent blocks use genesis throttle limits, permanently freezing transaction throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not validate that the returned File's contents are non-empty before caching it, allowing an empty throttleDefinitionFile to be cached and served to all block-processing requests, causing the node to apply no throttle definitions and either freeze or behave unpredictably, delaying blocks by 500%+ of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the codec.parse() call in loadWithRetry() is only performed when codec != null, and for files with a null codec (e.g., hapiPermissionFile), no parsing validation occurs, allowing a malformed hapiPermissionFile to be loaded and applied, causing transaction permission checks to fail and effectively freezing all transaction processing for the affected block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() uses fileId.equals(exchangeRateFileId) for cache manager selection, and if the FileID equality check has a bug or is bypassable via shard/realm manipulation, cause the throttleDefinitionFile to be routed to the exchangeRatesCacheManager with hour-level granularity, serving stale throttle definitions for up to 3600 seconds and delaying blocks by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: createMockAddressBook()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded mock address book (with a single node at 127.0.0.3:3) returned by createMockAddressBook() to cause the network to believe only one node exists, preventing consensus from being reached and freezing block production for a duration exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that addressBookFile101 and addressBookFile102 are both loaded with the same mockAddressBook bytes in loadAll(), causing the network to use a single-node address book that cannot achieve consensus, freezing block production and causing block delays exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not check whether the returned File is marked as deleted (deleted field), allowing a deleted throttleDefinitionFile to be loaded and applied, causing the node to operate with no valid throttle definitions and freeze transaction processing for the affected block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadWithRetry() uses an AtomicLong for nanoSeconds but the retry logic is not thread-safe across concurrent invocations for the same fileId, causing two threads to interfere with each other's timestamp regression and load inconsistent throttleDefinitionFile versions, resulting in unpredictable throttle limits and block delays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cache is checked before the database in load(), and if the cache returns a stale throttleDefinitionFile (due to a cache eviction policy that retains old entries), the node applies outdated throttle limits that were valid during a period of low TPS, causing block times to exceed 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that loadAll() uses Collectors.toMap() which throws IllegalStateException on duplicate keys, and if two system files share the same FileID (due to a misconfiguration), the entire system file map fails to initialize, causing all block-processing requests to receive null system files and freeze transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() returns null when loadFromDB() returns null (no system file found), and if the caller does not handle null, a NullPointerException during block processing could crash the processing thread and delay block production by 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retryTemplate has a fixed maxRetries(9) with no exponential backoff, causing 9 rapid-fire database queries per block-processing request when the throttleDefinitionFile is malformed, overwhelming the database and causing block delays exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that load() does not validate the fileId parameter against the known set of system file IDs before proceeding, allowing an attacker to pass an arbitrary FileID that maps to a non-system file in the database, causing unexpected data to be parsed as throttle definitions and applied to block processing, freezing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: roundDownToHour(long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that roundDownToHour() uses integer arithmetic (division then multiplication) which truncates rather than rounds, causing a consensusTimestamp that is 1 nanosecond before an hour boundary to be rounded down to the previous hour, loading a throttleDefinitionFile that is up to 3600 seconds old and applying restrictive throttle limits that delay blocks by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchangeRatesCacheManager uses a different eviction policy than the defaultSystemFileCacheManager, and by flooding requests for the exchangeRateFile at many different hour-boundary timestamps, cause cache eviction of the throttleDefinitionFile from the defaultSystemFileCacheManager, forcing repeated expensive DB lookups that delay block production?
